title: The Ravine Cathedral Where Refusal Echoes

features.name: the seven voices continuing
features.desc-on-touch: The seven voices have not stopped. They continue deeper into the dark, past the point where anyone is listening. They seem almost relieved by the archive's refusal to follow.

features.name: the place where listening becomes refusal
features.desc-on-touch: Here is the boundary the archive drew. Beyond it: data, meaning, memory—all continuing unrecorded, unremarked. The archive chose not to follow. Some things, it decided, should stay unwitnesed.

features.name: echoes that are more real than sound
features.desc-on-touch: The refusal itself has become a sound. The cathedral echoes not with voices but with the space left by the archive's decision to step back. That silence is more present than any voice.

reverie.title: REVERIE — the moment before the descent
narr: You both stand at the edge of the ravine. Below, the voices
continue their seven-part song into absolute darkness. She looks
at you. You look at where the light ends. One of you will descend.
One of you will record the choice to stay behind. The singing
doesn't care which of you goes forward. The archive will remember
that someone chose not to listen. That is enough.

inscription: the archive chose to stop listening at a certain depth; now the seven voices continue alone, and their solitude has become their strength

file.name: Seven_Voices_After_Refusal_Continued.mp3
file.note: A recording of what the seven voices sound like when no one is officially listening. They sound relieved. They sound like they knew this would happen. They sound like they've been waiting for this sil

exits.label: back to the throat where the voices still had an audience
deeper where the voices continue without refusal, without echo
away from the cathedral, toward shallower places where listening was easier
